title: Clinical Negligence

statuteRef.label: Special time limit for actions in respect of personal injuries (clinical negligence)

questions.label: Date of the negligent treatment or omission
questions.helpText: The date on which the clinical negligence occurred — for example, the date of the operation, diagnosis, or treatment that is alleged to have been negligent.

questions.label: Do you know the claimant's date of knowledge?
questions.helpText: Under s.14 Limitation Act 1980, the 'date of knowledge' is when the claimant first knew: (a) the injury was significant, (b) it was attributable to the defendant's act or omission, (c) the defendant's identity, and (d) supporting facts. In clinical negligence, patients may not realise treatment was substandard until much later.

questions.label: Date of knowledge
questions.helpText: The date the claimant first had (or ought reasonably to have had) knowledge that their injury was attributable to the clinical treatment received.

questions.label: Was the claimant under a disability at the date of accrual?
questions.helpText: Under the Limitation Act 1980, a person is under a disability if they are a minor (under 18) or lack capacity within the meaning of the Mental Capacity Act 2005. In clinical negligence, birth injury claims often involve child claimants.

questions.label: Date disability ceased
questions.helpText: The date the claimant turned 18, or the date capacity was regained. If disability is ongoing, select 'Unsure' above.

questions.label: Was there deliberate concealment of the negligence?
questions.helpText: Under s.32 Limitation Act 1980, if the defendant (or their employer) deliberately concealed relevant facts — for example, altering medical records — the limitation period does not begin until the claimant discovered or could with reasonable diligence have discovered the concealment.

questions.label: Date of discovery of concealment
questions.helpText: The date the claimant discovered, or could with reasonable diligence have discovered, the deliberate concealment.

notes: Clinical negligence follows the same limitation framework as personal injury under s.11, with the 3-year period running from the later of injury or knowledge.
The court has discretion under s.33 Limitation Act 1980 to disapply the time limit if it is equitable to do so.
Birth injury claims where the child lacks capacity may have no effective limitation period until capacity is regained (if ever).
The Pre-Action Protocol for the Resolution of Clinical Disputes should be followed before issuing proceedings.
